commit.message:
feat: 16 models via GitHub Models API — no API keys needed

All these work with just 'gh auth login':
- OpenAI: GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 Mini, GPT-4.1 Nano
- DeepSeek: R1 (reasoning)
- Meta: Llama 3.1 405B, Llama 3.3 70B, Llama 4 Scout
- Mistral: Codestral, Mistral Small
- Microsoft: Phi-4, Phi-4 Mini, Phi-4 Multimodal
- Cohere: Command R+, Command R

OpenRouter reduced to 4 models (Claude/Gemini only).
Removed fake model IDs that didn't exist on GitHub Models.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tokens): Sprint 28A — Foundation design tokens overhaul

- A1: WCAG orange fix (#f59e0b → #B45309, 5.02:1 contrast ratio)
- A2: Primary blue warmth (#3356D0 → #2563EB)
- A3: Mobile font size optimization + iOS zoom prevention
- A4: Background palette migrated to Slate family
- A5: Status 'used' color updated to slate palette
- A6: Letter-spacing design tokens (--tracking-wide, --tracking-wider)
- A7: Line-height design tokens (--leading-tight through --leading-compact)

QA fixes applied:
- H1: Added --color-danger-50 to @media dark block
- H2: Aligned Tailwind caption letterSpacing with token
- M1-M2: Heading line-heights now use CSS custom properties
- M4: Purged stale #f59e0b and #3356D0 from login, admin, tests
- M5: Warning bg-muted rgba aligned with --color-warning base
- L2: Added --color-status-used token for dynamic theming

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Kernel AI Assistant is a high-performance, local-first intelligent agent designed to bridge the gap between cloud-scale reasoning and total data sovereignty. Built specifically for the Android ecosystem, Kernel AI bypasses the cloud entirely, executing state-of-the-art Gemma-4 E-4B and E-2B models directly on your device's NPU and GPU.

* 🔖 Auto-update version to v2.1.91

* Fix README.md formatting - remove extra space in greeting

* 🔖 Auto-update version to v2.1.93

* Add comprehensive Feliz Jueves Mode post-game proposal

- Complete redesign of post-game content as 'Feliz Jueves Mode'
- New narrative centered around eternal Thursday survival
- Added Buen Finde points system and Friday levels
- Implemented Thursday cycle mechanics with random events
- Included technical implementation details and UI changes
- Fixed all Markdown linting errors

* 🔖 Auto-update version to v2.1.95

* feat: implement Feliz Jueves Mode post-game system (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement core Feliz Jueves Mode post-game system

- Add Thursday Mode variables and events system
- Implement unlock condition after completing game
- Add Thursday cycle with time progression
- Create Buen Finde points system with 5 initial events
- Add Thursday Mode UI panel with clock and progress bars
- Integrate with existing game loop and save system
- Add CSS styling for Thursday Mode panel
- Update help command with Thursday Mode instructions

Co-authored-by: Lubonch <39447963+Lubonch@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback for Thursday Mode

- Add named constants for magic numbers (event intervals, probabilities, retention ratio)
- Fix mail cooldown effect to extend instead of replace
- Replace setTimeout with persistent timestamp for Friday end time
- Add check in game loop to verify Friday expiration across sessions
- Save/load fridayEndTime to maintain state across browser sessions

Co-authored-by: Lubonch <39447963+Lubonch@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: hide main credits when Thursday Mode is active, add Buen Finde credits

- Hide regular credits section when Thursday Mode panel is displayed
- Add new "Buen Finde" credits section for post-game completion
- Credits automatically hide/show when toggling Thursday Mode
- Buen Finde credits appear after reaching Friday Level 5
- Restore regular credits when Thursday Mode is deactivated

Co-authored-by: Lubonch <39447963+Lubonch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix credits display and Thursday Mode activation logic

- Fix credits hiding logic: show credits when game is completed, hide only when Thursday Mode is ACTIVE (not just unlocked)

- Remove call to non-existent initializeThursdayMode function
- Improve logic flow for post-game credit display

* 🔖 Auto-update version to v2.1.106

* fix: format package.json and update version in index.html

* Modularizes game logic and adds automated test coverage

Separates core game logic into a reusable engine module to enable isolated unit testing and future UI migrations. Integrates Vitest for automated testing, achieving initial coverage of key mechanics and serialization. Updates scripts and dependencies to support modern JS modules and test workflows. Lays groundwork for robust, maintainable development and facilitates eventual Angular migration as outlined in the new implementation plan.

* Remove debug logs and update dev instructions

Cleans up code by removing numerous debug console statements related to dungeon and control logic, resulting in cleaner output and improved maintainability. Updates the documentation to include clear instructions for installing dependencies, running tests, and generating coverage reports, making development and testing workflows more accessible.

* .

* feat: add .gitignore and refactor game state management in game.js

* refactor: remove coverage report, add coffee production logic, and update tests

- Deleted the coverage report file as it is no longer needed.
- Introduced a new function `produceCoffee` in `game-engine.js` to handle coffee production logic.
- Updated `game.js` to utilize the new `produceCoffee` function and refactored validation logic.
- Removed the old `validateGameValues` function and replaced its calls with a new validation approach.
- Enhanced tests in `game-engine.test.js` to cover the new coffee production functionality and ensure state persistence after upgrades.

* feat: initialize Angular application with core structure and game engine

- Added package.json for Angular app setup with dependencies and scripts.
- Included favicon.ico for application branding.
- Created app configuration file for global error handling.
- Developed main application component with HTML and CSS.
- Implemented game engine service and logic for managing game state.
- Defined game state interfaces and initial values for upgrades and bosses.
- Set up TypeScript configuration files for application and testing.
- Added index.html as the entry point for the Angular application.
- Updated game.js to include bosses in the engine state.

* feat: implement Angular migration with game engine integration, UI components, and state management

* feat: add new game panels and enhance save/load functionality with state management

* feat: add GitHub Actions workflow for CI and automated deployment to GitHub Pages

* chore: remove outdated implementation plan document

* feat: implement GameStateService for state management and update GameEngineService to utilize it

commit.message:
Fix autotuner worker device affinity (#104)

* Fix autotuner worker OOM by restricting workers to parent's CUDA device

In multi-rank training, autotuner subprocess workers all initialize a CUDA
context on device 0 (the default). When many ranks on the same node spawn
workers simultaneously, the CUDA context overhead on GPU 0 causes OOM.

Fix by setting CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES in the worker environment to the parent
rank's current device. The logical device index is mapped through the parent's
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES to handle GPU UUIDs and MIG IDs.

We guard with torch.cuda.is_initialized() to avoid eagerly initializing CUDA
and to handle the case where autotuning runs before device selection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: extract _get_current_cuda_device() helper

Move the logic for resolving the current physical CUDA device identifier
into a standalone function that returns None when the device cannot be
determined.

fix: skip Unix permission assertions on Windows in updater tests

Windows does not support Unix-style file permission bits. os.Stat on
Windows always reports 0666 (or 0444 for read-only), so asserting
0755 or 0700 always fails. Guard the permission checks with a
runtime.GOOS != "windows" condition. The core replacement logic
(content, .old cleanup) remains tested on all platforms.

[fix](fe) Fix no_use_cbo_rule hint being silently ignored in EliminateLogicalSelectHint (#62358)

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:
When a query uses the `/*+ no_use_cbo_rule(RULE_NAME) */` hint, the hint
was silently ignored. The root cause was that
`EliminateLogicalSelectHint#build` only handled the `USE_CBO_RULE` hint
name but had no branch for `NO_USE_CBO_RULE`. As a result, the hint fell
into the else branch which just logged a warning and skipped it, so the
CBO rule was never disabled.

**Fix:** add `NO_USE_CBO_RULE` to the existing `USE_CBO_RULE` condition
so both hint names are routed to `extractRule()`, which already handles
both cases via the `isNotUseCboRule` flag on `SelectHintUseCboRule`.

Example query that was broken before this fix:
```sql
explain physical plan
SELECT /*+ no_use_cbo_rule(INFER_SET_OPERATOR_DISTINCT)*/ ka, kb, kc 
FROM t1 INTERSECT SELECT ka, kb, kc FROM t2
```

### Release note

Fix: `no_use_cbo_rule` hint now correctly disables the specified CBO
rewrite rule instead of being silently ignored.

commit.message:
tui: fix two separator bugs found by code review

Bug 1 (high): token display was ambiguous — produced '4k1k' instead of
'4k↑ 1k↓'. fmt_tokens() only returns the number; arrows must be added
at the call site. Fixed format string to use ↑/↓ between input/output.

Bug 2 (medium): separator content could overflow narrow terminals.
Added truncation: info string is capped so that info + scroll fits

[refactor](fe) Replace all JMockit usage with Mockito and remove JMockit dependency (#62221)

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary: 
1. Update FE source and target version to 17
2. Replace all JMockit usage in FE unit tests with Mockito and remove
JMockit dependency

The FE unit tests used JMockit (1.49) as a mocking framework alongside
Mockito (4.11.0), creating maintenance complexity and potential agent
conflicts (e.g., with JaCoCo). This PR replaces **all** JMockit usage
across ~243 test files with Mockito equivalents and removes the JMockit
dependency entirely.

### Changes

**Migration patterns applied:**
- `@Mocked T field` → `T field = Mockito.mock(T.class)`
- `@Injectable T param` → `T param = Mockito.mock(T.class)`
- `new Expectations() {{ method; result=v; }}` →
`when(method).thenReturn(v)` / `doReturn(v).when(mock).method()`
- `MockUp<T>` → `MockedStatic<T>`, `MockedConstruction<T>`, or
`Mockito.spy()`
- `new Verifications() {{ m(); times=n; }}` → `verify(mock,
times(n)).m()`

**Dependency removal:**
- Removed `jmockit` version property and dependency from `fe/pom.xml`
- Removed `-javaagent:jmockit` from surefire plugin in
`fe/fe-core/pom.xml` and `max-compute-connector/pom.xml`

**Note:** Custom `Deencapsulation` utility retained (not from JMockit
jar).

修正：PermissionError 處理與 retry_interval_cycles=1 無效問題

- conv_admin.py：將 PermissionError 從 ProcessLookupError 分開 catch；
  PermissionError 表示 process 存在但由其他用戶擁有，應將 _daemon_running=True，
  而非 fallback 到 .env，與 BaseDaemon.check_pid_file() 行為一致
- process_daemon.py：retry 條件從 == 1 改為 == 0，使 RETRY_INTERVAL_CYCLES=1
  能正確在每個 cycle 觸發重試；原條件 n%1==1 永遠為 false，導致重試完全失效

* fix(ci): fix Rust crate packaging — use workspace root with -p agentmesh

cargo package in a workspace writes .crate files to the workspace root's
target/package/, not the individual crate's directory. The pipeline was
running from the crate subdirectory and couldn't find the output.

Fix: change workingDirectory from packages/agent-mesh/sdks/rust/agentmesh
to packages/agent-mesh/sdks/rust (workspace root) and add -p agentmesh
to all cargo commands to target the specific crate.

commit.message:
fix: enter key sends CR (0x0D) not LF (0x0A) for remote sessions

KEY_MAP['enter'] was '10' (LF) but terminals expect '13' (CR).
This caused submit to fail in copilot TUI on remote nodes — the LF
went to the display area instead of submitting the input.
KEY_BYTES already had the correct b'\r'.
